programme.title: Classified
programme.desc: Rachel's return to Canada changes everything for her entourage. The hunt for the mole takes centre stage, which leaves the Shammarian agents the freedom to work on their own plans. Rachel and Emile's family life crumbles and each must do their utmost to survive. McKenna imposes his conditions on the CIA to protect the \.

programme.title: Classified
programme.desc: Rachel's past comes to light and the hunt for the mole grows more frantic. The Shammarian secret service agents go forward with their plan and all the pieces of the puzzle come together. Irene gets her revenge. The Chadli face hard times, as do Rachel and Emile's family.

programme.title: Legacy List
programme.desc: After spending years taking care of her family a woman finally gets the chance to move into a dream house of her own. The problem is she only has a week to move.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: Murrumbidgee River - Madi Madi, Dadi Dadi & Nganguruku Country
programme.desc: A slow TV showcase of the stunning landscapes found in Madi Madi, Dadi Dadi and Nganguruku Country along the waters of the Murrumbidgee River.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.sub-title: Sweet Potato and White Fungus Soup
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC 6 O'Clock News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: Writer Ankit has a way with words but will that help him in the search for a bride, while a dinner party unites some of our arranged favourites to discuss the highs and lows of their arranged marriage experience.

programme.title: Spectacular Railways Of The Swiss Alps
programme.desc: For over 130 years, the cogwheel trains of the Schynige Platte Railway have been climbing leisurely into the Swiss Alps, and little has changed. This is a journey back in time through picture-perfect alpine landscapes, past families who have worked these mountains for generations, and alongside the dedicated crew who keep this century-old railway running. The destination promises one of Switzerland's most spectacular panoramas.

programme.title: Along Ancient Tracks
programme.desc: Winding through two of Romania's most breathtaking national parks, the Anina railway traces a remarkable path along steep slopes and into the untouched wilderness of the Banat Mountains. Where mining and industry once carved tunnels through ancient karst stone, nature has reclaimed its wild beauty. Locals have long fought to save this historic line - and we travel its length at just 17 km per hour, uncovering the stories that live along Romania's oldest mountain railway.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: Destination Flavour: Japan Bitesize
programme.desc: Some of your favourite moments and recipes from Adam Liaw's culinary and cultural journey through Japan, from its frozen north to cherry-blossomed mainland and tropical sun-soaked south.

programme.title: Cotswolds and Beyond with Pam Ayres
programme.desc: In the opening leg of Pam's tour of the Cotswolds, she starts off in the picturesque village of Bourton-on-the-Water. It's a place where the River Windrush flows through the centre of the village, earning it the title 'the Venice of the Cotswolds'. It's here Pam soaks up the scenery that attracts thousands of tourists every year - along with a Cotswold cream tea - before visiting Andy Wallington's antique shop, and then a world famous perfumery that's made scents for the Queen.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.sub-title: Rachael Maza - A Life In Theatre
programme.desc: Rachael Maza is one of Australia's most enduring and compelling First Nations storytellers. Having departed the Ilbijerri Theatre Company, she shares what's next in her remarkable career.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.desc: Guillaume creates his dad's favourite cake, the Paris Brest. This light and airy dessert was created in honour of another famous French cycling race.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Two of Australia's finest Indian cuisine experts, Justin Narayan and Bhavna Shivalkar, join Adam for a night of fast, fresh Indian food.

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists hunt for clues to how the queens of ancient Egypt ruled in a society dominated by men. They enter an unexplored pyramid in search of the burial chamber of a mysterious queen who lived 4500 years ago. They investigate how one of the few women to ever rule as a Pharaoh rose to power, and uncover the surprising role queen Nefertari played in the reign of Ramses the Great.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Tonight, The Aeneid, Anne of Green Gables, K-POP's NCT and Rowan Atkinson's Mr Bean. Grab your teddy bear, stick a raw turkey on your head and get ready to quiz. Contenders: Duncan Hames, Theo Josseff, Nola Passmore, Hannah Shayer.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein retraces his first steps in Sydney, reconnecting with old friends and exciting new influences to explore how the culinary landscape is evolving.

programme.title: Secrets of Flying Scotsman
programme.desc: Episode two continues the story as businessman Alan Pegler, who saved Scotsman from the scrapheap, took it on an ambitious 1969 tour of America to promote British industry. However mounting debts bankrupted him. There wasn't even enough money to bring Scotsman back to Britain and it looked like it would be lost forever. Today, more than a century after it was built, painstaking restoration means the engine is still steaming across the UK - and enchanting a whole new generation of fans.

programme.title: Riot Women
programme.desc: While Kitty spends the night in police custody with a broken hand, Beth and Tom have a tragic, terminal conversation about her. Jess has a massive hangover and forgets what she agreed to the night before. Holly visits Nisha in hospital and gets a disturbing shock. Jenny Bollocks wants to manage the Riot Women! But is there enough good will left to move forward?

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Under The Bridge
programme.desc: Upsetting news shakes the small town of Victoria, and as rumours surface, Rebecca and Cam reconnect. In the past, Reena enters Josephine's mafia fantasy world.

programme.title: The Unusual Suspects
programme.desc: As Sara Beasley is set to make the deal of a lifetime, her 'perfect' life is secretly about to come crashing down and her selfish ways might cause her devoted nanny, Evie, to lose everything too.

programme.title: The Unusual Suspects
programme.desc: Sara and Evie hatch a cunning plan to cover up Sara's indiscretions with Nick and save her multimillion-dollar deal, while Roxanne's world is shattered when Jordan is arrested for fraud.

programme.title: Beyond Signs
programme.desc: Romain gets a clear understanding that Diego's visions are linked to unsolved crime scenes. The dead turn to him to help deliver the message of finding them.

programme.title: The Lost Camps of The Third Reich
programme.desc: A Nazi labour camp on British territory? Along with many others in equally shocking places. Now, hidden in the landscape, these forgotten camps imprisoned hundreds of thousands of detainees who were forced to toil for the Nazi terror machine, building massive structures for the 3rd Reich like the mysterious and unfinished Riese project, or the Arctic highway known as the 'Blood Road'.

programme.title: Legacy List
programme.desc: A Brooklyn couple need help clearing out a basement full of memories. The house is filled with items that not just tell their story but the story of the neighbourhood they love.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: A slow TV showcase of the stunning landscapes found in Arrernte Country.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC 6 O'Clock News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: 25-year-old Seerat and 23-year-old Niki are both ready to find a life partner. In Nelson, gun enthusiast Seerat is handing over the decision on who he will marry to his parents. When they finally find a perfect match in India can a gun-themed proposal help them hit their target? Meanwhile, Auckland-based Niki and her family have hatched a plan to find 'Mr Perfect' and everyone has an opinion.

programme.title: Hidden Beauty of Northern France
programme.sub-title: The Baie de Somme and the Opal Coast
programme.desc: It is a rich region with a stunning coastline and diverse landscapes and wildlife: Northern France, the area between Belgium and the English Channel, has so much to offer.

programme.title: Along Ancient Tracks
programme.desc: Built by the Austrian Empire in the early 20th century to connect Vienna with the Adriatic Sea, the Bohinj Railway winds through some of Slovenia's most breathtaking scenery - rivers, mountains, and the stunning valleys of the Julian Alps. Once a dividing line between nations and ideologies, it has become a cultural and historical crossroads. Today, the route passes through a UNESCO-protected Biosphere Reserve, home to bears, wolves, chamois, deer, and marmots.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: Destination Flavour: Japan Bitesize
programme.desc: Some of your favourite moments and recipes from Adam Liaw's culinary and cultural journey through Japan, from its frozen north to cherry-blossomed mainland and tropical sun-soaked south.

programme.title: Cotswolds and Beyond with Pam Ayres
programme.desc: Pam's journey starts in Wiltshire and the town of Bradford on Avon as she takes to the water on a narrowboat. Then she meets the beekeeper producing delicious Cotswold honeycomb before heading to the ancient wool town of Tetbury, and discovers why it's become one of the best places in Britain to buy antiques.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.desc: Guillaume shows us a traditional Breton dessert that is all about enjoying life to the fullest. The Kouign-amann is known as the \.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.desc: Brittany is a region that is celebrated for its abundant seafood. Lorient is home to the second largest fishing port in France. Guillaume is meeting with one of Australia's best spear fishermen to cook some freshly caught rock lobster on a wood fire.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: No need to adjust your wireless - Adam and his ARIA award-winning guests, Guy Sebastian and Sarah McLeod, have got you covered with nonstop weeknight hits!

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists hunt for clues to explain one of Ancient Egypt's great mysteries - the rise of mummification. They investigate what could be one of the oldest mummies ever found, and break into an intact tomb to learn how mummification endured beyond the end of Egyptian civilization.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Our weekly family winners are back with two minutes of general knowledge, plus the dreaded slow burn round. Tonight's slow burn topics are a Film, a City, an Animal and a Constellation.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie meets a sculptor creating wildlife out of scrap metal and gets a tour of the region with a passionate local. Rae cruises the Clarence River with a Yaegl artist to learn how it inspires her work.

programme.title: A New ANZAC
programme.desc: Crowds of over 30,000 people gather at the Australian War Memorial for the 2025 Anzac Day Ceremony. Marching in the parade for the first time since becoming a double amputee, is Royal Australian Navy veteran Luke McCallum. The C130 Hercules aircraft moves to its new home in the memorial. Also making the careful journey is the ten and a half tonne Chinook Helicopter, which is towed 40 kms over eight hours to its new display.

programme.title: Orient Express: A Golden Era of Travel
programme.desc: In the Golden Age of Rail, a ticket from London to Istanbul on the Orient Express network meant crossing continents in style. Beyond the Balkans, Sofia offers a stark reminder of a country with a troubled past. Further south, a royal palace hides exotic surprises in its grounds. The train's final destination: Istanbul, where East meets West, and the unexpected always feels possible.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: The King
programme.desc: Bruno temporarily empties the Islamic wing and fills it with spy micro-cameras. Laura has a trojan installed in Bruno's phone to inquire on his entire life: past and present. In the Islamic wing Bruno finds a counterfeit book: it contains a message extolling the jihad. There could be a radicaliser among the inmates. Farid is given into the care of Aniello and becomes his pupil; but there's something suspicious about him. Gloria distances herself from Bruno.

programme.title: Babylon Berlin
programme.desc: In urgent need of money, Charlotte enters a lucrative dance marathon; Councillor Wendt pitches his plans to Nyssen; Rath visits Stennes in prison.

programme.title: Babylon Berlin
programme.desc: Abraham Goldstein arrives in Berlin as the secret organisation White Hand passes a verdict on a criminal, Rath investigates a boxing match.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: It's the weekend shift for on-call vets Rosalind and Aidan in the busy spring calving season. USPCA wildlife officer Phil responds to a couple of sick swans that need rescuing.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vets Alanna and Lorna need to operate on a three legged Lurcher but make a worrying discovery. Vet Martin must try to save twin lambs and Gillian anaesthetises a goldfish.

programme.title: Legacy List
programme.desc: A retired entertainment executive needs Matt's help clearing out a basement full of showbiz memorabilia. The collection includes autographs, posters and rare pictures of Hollywood's Golden Age.
